papers.title: Family of magnetic field-boosted superconductors in rhombohedral graphene
papers.body_text: In some unconventional superconductors, time-reversal symmetry can be broken in addition to the gauge symmetry1, resulting in superconductivities that can be enhanced or induced by magnetic fields2. However, field-enhanced superconductors are more vulnerable to impurities than Bardeen-Cooper-Schrieffer counterparts3. Crystalline rhombohedral multilayer graphene is a promising platform to explore them due to its superior material quality and gate-tunable strong correlation effects4,5. Here we report transport measurements of rhombohedral tetralayer and pentalayer graphene, demonstrating a spectrum of clean-limit superconductivities. We found three different types of field-enhanced and field-induced superconductivities in the pentalayer. They are all robust against an in-plane field up to 8.5 Tesla, exceeding the Pauli limit by tens of times. Compared to Bernal bilayer graphene showing only in-plane field-enhancement6, pentalayer graphene features superconductors enhanced by out-of-plane as well as in-plane fields. They also reside at much lower gate electric fields owing to the intrinsically flatter band dispersion—facilitating their study and further engineering. Additionally, we observed that proximitized spin-orbit coupling (SOC) generates multiple new superconductors without introducing additional disorder effects. Our work establishes a new family of magnetic field-boosted superconductors in rhombohedral graphene. Utilizing the high accessibility with moderate gate voltages, this will pave the way for realizing non-Abelian quasiparticles through interfacial engineering7 in the extreme clean limit, in that proximitized SOC leads to topological states8 and maintains the ultrahigh quality of crystalline graphene.

questions.stem: In some unconventional superconductors, time-reversal symmetry can be _____ in addition to the gauge symmetry.
questions.explanation: 上下文指出时间反转对称性在非常规超导体中是被破坏的，broken符合语境。

questions.stem: These superconductivities can be _____ or induced by magnetic fields.
questions.explanation: 根据后文field-enhanced，此处应填入增强，即enhanced。

questions.stem: However, field-enhanced superconductors are more _____ to impurities than Bardeen-Cooper-Schrieffer counterparts.
questions.explanation: vulnerable意为易受伤害的，符合材料科学中超导体对杂质敏感的语境。

questions.stem: Crystalline rhombohedral multilayer graphene is a promising _____ to explore them due to its superior material quality.
questions.explanation: 石墨烯被视为探索超导现象的实验基础，即平台(platform)。

questions.stem: It is also chosen due to its gate-tunable strong _____ effects.
questions.explanation: 强关联(correlation)效应是凝聚态物理中的专业术语。

questions.stem: Here we report transport _____ of rhombohedral tetralayer and pentalayer graphene.
questions.explanation: 实验报告通常涉及具体的测量数据(measurements)。

questions.stem: The study demonstrates a _____ of clean-limit superconductivities.
questions.explanation: spectrum在此处指代一系列或广泛的范围。

questions.stem: We found three different types of field-enhanced and field-induced superconductivities in the _____.
questions.explanation: 前文明确提到了研究对象为pentalayer石墨烯。

questions.stem: They are all _____ against an in-plane field up to 8.5 Tesla.
questions.explanation: robust意为强健的，指代超导性在磁场下依然稳定。

questions.stem: The magnetic field strength _____ the Pauli limit by tens of times.
questions.explanation: exceeds意为超过，符合描述磁场强度突破极限的语境。

questions.stem: Compared to Bernal bilayer graphene showing only in-plane field-enhancement, pentalayer graphene _____ superconductors enhanced by out-of-plane fields.
questions.explanation: features意为以...为特色，符合语境。

questions.stem: They also reside at much lower gate electric fields _____ to the intrinsically flatter band dispersion.
questions.explanation: owing to意为由于，是标准的因果逻辑衔接词。

questions.stem: The flatter band dispersion is _____ to their study and further engineering.
questions.explanation: flatter band利于研究，故使用facilitating(促进)。

questions.stem: Additionally, we observed that proximitized spin-orbit coupling (SOC) _____ multiple new superconductors.
questions.explanation: SOC产生了新的超导体，故用generates。

questions.stem: This occurs without introducing additional _____ effects.
questions.explanation: 物理实验中通常强调不引入额外的杂乱(disorder)。

questions.stem: Our work establishes a new _____ of magnetic field-boosted superconductors.
questions.explanation: family在这里指代一类或一系列发现。

questions.stem: Utilizing the high _____ with moderate gate voltages...
questions.explanation: accessibility指实验条件容易达到，符合gate voltages的语境。

questions.stem: This will _____ the way for realizing non-Abelian quasiparticles.
questions.explanation: pave the way是一个固定搭配，意为铺平道路。

questions.stem: This is possible through _____ engineering in the extreme clean limit.
questions.explanation: interfacial engineering(界面工程)是材料科学术语。

questions.stem: Proximitized SOC leads to topological states and _____ the ultrahigh quality of crystalline graphene.
questions.explanation: SOC导致了拓扑态且保持了石墨烯的高质量，故选maintains。

papers.title: Christine Lagarde’s sober tone on the Gulf war energy shock
papers.body_text: Christine Lagarde’s sober tone on the Gulf war energy shock Finance & economics | Navigating a Trumpian world Christine Lagarde’s sober tone on the Gulf war energy shock Our interview with the ECB’s president March 26th 2026 THE RISKS from the Iran war, says Christine Lagarde, are being underestimated. Amid what the International Energy Agency calls the biggest energy shock ever, the president of the European Central Bank says expectations of a swift return to normal may be “overly optimistic”. “We are facing a real shock…probably beyond what we can imagine at the moment.” Speaking to The Economist at the ECB’s headquarters for “The Insider” , our video show, on March 25th, Ms Lagarde gave a sober view of the risks to the world economy. The bank’s technical experts believe “too much has already been damaged”, she said, and that there is “no way” the Gulf’s lost energy supply can be restored within months. The disruption may last “years”. The danger, she argued, is that the consequences will emerge only gradually, leading to a “delayed assessment” of how serious the crisis is. That struck a more pessimistic tone than a speech Ms Lagarde gave earlier the same day, when she said it was “too early to say” which scenario for the energy shock would unfold. Still, on monetary policy, she said the ECB was “well positioned to respond”. She is more concerned about government budgets. “There is not as much fiscal space” as in 2022-23, when countries spent 2.5% of GDP or more to cushion energy costs after Russia’s invasion of Ukraine. Any support this time should be “tailored, targeted and temporary”. Policymakers should be mindful of how “broad a blanket” they deploy, and focus on low-income households. On America’s role in the global system, Ms Lagarde was notably more critical. Asked whether countries could come together in a global crisis, she quipped: “I can assure you that the central-bank community will get together,” pointedly omitting finance ministers. She noted that during the financial crisis of 2007-09 the G20 co-ordinated a global response. “Can you imagine that happening now? The G20 is under US leadership. I’ll say no more.” She detects echoes of the 1920s, when America turned inward. Yet she is only partially a “Carneyist”—agreeing with Mark Carney, Canada’s prime minister, that the old order is breaking down, but resisting the idea that it must be rebuilt from scratch. Multilateral institutions such as the IMF, World Bank and World Trade Organisation, she said, can still be repaired—even if America’s “dominance” within them must diminish. As with a snake shedding its skin, it is possible to “extract from the old skin a new animal”. In that world, Ms Lagarde argued, Europe still has a central role to play. “Europe-bashing is vastly excessive,” she said, suggesting that disappointment reflects high expectations. The continent, once a “dream” and a “promise”, has been a “brutal success” in securing peace. Yet some of the successes she cited, such as the Erasmus student-exchange programme, seem underwhelming. She acknowledges that Europe must do more. Her priorities, she insists, are clear: a true single market; deeper, more efficient capital markets—“you need the money”—to lift weak productivity and innovation; a digital euro to support integration. However, the recently proposed “28th regime”, a unified rule book, “lacks ambition”. But achieving any of this will require political cohesion, which is complicated by the rise of populist nationalism. Ms Lagarde brushed aside suggestions from figures such as Jordan Bardella, of France’s National Rally, that the ECB could tailor policy to individual countries. There is “no chance in the world” of that. The deeper risk, she suggested, is political. Europeans “will have to decide again whether they’re together or not”. Europe’s shared commitments to the rule of law, democracy and common values are under strain. “Where we massively failed,” she said, is in neglecting distribution, leaving large parts of society feeling left behind. If that is not fixed, Europe’s economic agenda may matter less than its political survival. ■ For more expert analysis of the biggest stories in economics, finance and markets, sign up to Money Talks , our weekly subscriber-only newsletter. This article was downloaded by zlibrary from https://www.economist.com//finance-and-economics/2026/03/26/christine-lagardes-sober-tone-on-the-gulf-war-energy-shock

questions.stem: According to the passage, what is Ms. Lagarde’s primary concern regarding the current energy shock?

options.A: The immediate collapse of global financial institutions.
options.B: The underestimation of the long-term, gradual impact of the crisis.
options.C: The inability of the European Central Bank to adjust monetary policy.
options.D: The lack of support from international energy agencies.

questions.explanation: 文章明确提到Ms. Lagarde认为当前的能源危机被低估了（underestimated），且该危机的后果将是渐进式显现的（emerge only gradually），导致评估滞后（delayed assessment）。

questions.stem: What does Ms. Lagarde imply by stating that support measures should be "tailored, targeted and temporary"?

options.A: Governments must increase spending to match the 2022-23 levels.
options.B: Energy subsidies should be provided indiscriminately to all citizens.
options.C: Fiscal policies must be precise and limited due to constrained budgets.
options.D: The ECB should take over fiscal responsibilities from national governments.

questions.explanation: 文章指出由于各国当前的财政空间（fiscal space）不如2022-23年，政府必须在提供支持时更加谨慎，需集中于低收入家庭，即财政支持应是精准且受限的。

questions.stem: How does Ms. Lagarde characterize the current state of global coordination, specifically regarding the G20?

options.A: She believes the G20 is still an effective platform for crisis resolution.
options.B: She implies that the G20 is paralyzed by US leadership and internal divisions.
options.C: She suggests that finance ministers are successfully leading global efforts.
options.D: She claims the central-bank community has been replaced by the G20.

questions.explanation: 当被问及全球协作时，Lagarde暗示G20在美方领导下已难以达成共识，并特意省略了财政部长，只提及央行间的合作，暗示政治层面的协作受阻。

questions.stem: What is Ms. Lagarde’s stance on the future of multilateral institutions like the IMF and World Bank?

options.A: They must be completely dismantled to prevent a return to 1920s-style isolationism.
options.B: They are beyond repair and need to be replaced by regional organizations.
options.C: They should function exactly as they did in the past without any changes.
options.D: They can be reformed and revitalized even with a reduction in US dominance.

questions.explanation: 文中提到Lagarde认为多边机构仍可修复（can still be repaired），即便这意味着美国在其中的支配地位（dominance）必须削弱。

questions.stem: Which of the following does Ms. Lagarde identify as a fundamental threat to Europe's long-term survival?

options.A: The excessive focus on Erasmus exchange programs.
options.B: The lack of ambition in the digital euro initiative.
options.C: The failure to address social inequality and political distribution.
options.D: The refusal of France’s National Rally to cooperate with the ECB.

questions.explanation: 文中明确指出欧洲失败的地方在于忽视了财富分配（neglecting distribution），导致社会大部分群体感到被抛弃，这若不解决，欧洲的经济议程将次于其政治生存问题。

papers.title: Pope Leo visits Algeria in sign of Africa’s growing importance to Catholic church
papers.body_text: Pope Leo XIV has arrived in Algeria for the first papal visit to the country, calling for peace on the opening stop of a tour of Africa that signals the continent’s growing importance to the Catholic church. The 11-day trip, which will include stops in Cameroon, Angola and Equatorial Guinea, is the longest by Pope Leo since being elected to the papacy in May last year. The choice to visit Africa sends a powerful signal that the continent is one of the church’s top priorities, according to academics and theologians. Adriaan van Klinken, a professor of religion and African studies at the University of Leeds, said this reflected shifting demographics, with Africa home to one of the fastest-growing Catholic populations and accounting for about 20% of Catholics worldwide. By contrast, the Catholic population in western Europe is in decline. “Africa is the site of vitality, of growth, of the future of the church,” Van Klinken said. In the last year alone, 14 new dioceses have been created across Africa, with the Catholic population growing by 7 million, according to John Pontifex, from the Catholic charity Aid to the Church in Need UK. “A focus on Africa this early on in Pope Leo’s pontificate no doubt reflects a sense that in terms of Catholicism this is a continent that is coming of age,” he said. The pope, on arrival at Algiers international airport on Monday, was welcomed by Algeria’s president, Abdelmadjid Tebboune. He was later taken to the Maqam Echahid, a monument that commemorates those who died in the 1954-62 Algerian war for independence against French colonial rule. Father Peter Claver Kogh, the rector of the Basilica of Our Lady of Africa in Algiers, described the visit as a moment to strengthen bonds between Christian and Muslim communities, and solidify “the desire to have a climate of peace and tolerance among these two religions”. He added: “That is what the world needs now – a world of fraternal living and living in harmony. That will be the utmost importance of this visit for Christians and Muslims who are here, and all those who desire to live in peace and harmony.” View image in fullscreen Pope Leo visits the Great Mosque Of Algiers with the mosque’s rector, Mohammed al-Mamoun al-Qasimi al-Hassani. Photograph: Guglielmo Mangiapane/Reuters For Austen Ivereigh, a biographer of Pope Francis, the trip signals continuity with his predecessor’s priorities. In 2019, Francis broke new ground with the joint “human fraternity” document signed with leading Muslim figures. “Leo will want to continue that all-important alliance in building a new world order of peace,” Ivereigh said. Pontifex said the trip was not just about interfaith relations, but also a sign that the pope remained committed to freedom of religion and belief. “His visit comes at a time when religious freedom in Algeria, be it for Christians, Ahmadi Muslims and more liberal Muslims, has declined in recent years, according to our research.” The trip has also been viewed as an opportunity to spotlight communities with long histories of injustice and exploitation who are often overlooked by the west. Lucy Esipila, the regional coordinator for Caritas Africa, said she believed the pope’s visit would have a profound impact on Catholic communities in the region. “At a time when many African nations continue to face conflict, debt burdens, and widening inequalities, this apostolic journey is a powerful expression of synodality, of ‘walking together’ as a global church that listens to voices from the peripheries.” Algeria is the only Muslim-majority country on the pope’s tour. While its Catholic population is relatively small, the country holds particular significance for Pope Leo as the birthplace of Saint Augustine. Leo is the first pontiff from the Augustinian order, a theological tradition that emphasises a commitment to “live together in harmony”. Prof Anna Rowlands, the holder of the St Hilda chair in Catholic social thought and practice at Durham University, said: “Starting his visit in Algeria shows the other side of African Christianity that Leo is also deeply attuned to: its ancient legacy.” North Africa was home to some of the earliest Christian communities before the arrival of Islam and remains central to the church’s intellectual and theological heritage. Rowlands added that as the former head of the Augustinian order, Pope Leo, then Friar Robert Prevost, travelled frequently to African communities. “The church in Africa is well known to him – probably better known than to any pope in the modern era.” The decision to make these African countries the focus of his longest trip so far as pope comes alongside his decision not to visit the US. “That’s the unspoken part of this,” said Dr Miles Pattenden, a historian of the Catholic church at the University of Oxford. Pope Leo not only declined an invitation to the US, Pattenden said, but on 4 July, American independence day, he will be making a visit to the Italian island of Lampedusa, which is the place of arrival for many Africans making the perilous journey across the Mediterranean. “He’s sending an extremely powerful message, which President Trump obviously understands, and that may explain some of his bombastic criticisms of the pope over the past few days,” Pattenden said. That contrast appears to speak directly to the communities Leo is seeking to reach. “It’s a feeling of joy,” Father Kogh said of hearing Leo address the people of Algeria. “I’m so glad to have heard that message, because it was what I was expecting: a message of peace, and a call to coexistence and living in fraternity. So my joy redoubles.”

questions.stem: According to the text, why is Pope Leo's choice to visit Africa considered a "powerful signal" by academics?

options.A: It highlights the historical decline of the church in western Europe.
options.B: It marks the first time a pope has visited the continent of Africa.
options.C: It acknowledges Africa's status as a top priority due to its growing Catholic population.
options.D: It demonstrates a shift in the church's focus away from its traditional theological roots.

questions.explanation: 文章第三段明确提到，选择访问非洲发出了一个强有力的信号，即非洲是教会的首要任务之一（one of the church’s top priorities），原因在于非洲拥有世界上增长最快的天主教人口。

questions.stem: What is the primary significance of Pope Leo's visit to Algeria in the context of his own background?

options.A: Algeria is the center of the largest Catholic community in North Africa.
options.B: It is the birthplace of Saint Augustine, whose theological order the Pope belonged to.
options.C: The Pope aims to rectify historical injustices committed during French colonial rule.
options.D: It serves as a base to influence liberal Muslims in the region.

questions.explanation: 文章倒数第五段提到，阿尔及利亚对教皇利奥有特殊意义，因为它是圣奥古斯丁的诞生地，而利奥正是来自奥古斯丁教派。

questions.stem: How does the author characterize the 'unspoken' message regarding the Pope's decision to visit Lampedusa instead of the US?

options.A: As a strategic diplomatic move to strengthen US-Italian relations.
options.B: As a criticism of the US government's recent economic policies.
options.C: As an attempt to distance the church from the influence of global political leaders.
options.D: As a clear contrast in priorities by focusing on the plight of migrants rather than Western power.

questions.explanation: 文章最后一段指出，教皇拒绝了美国邀请，转而访问兰佩杜萨岛（移民抵达地），这与他向美国传递的信息形成鲜明对比，体现了对边缘群体的关注而非对西方强权的侧重。

questions.stem: What does the term 'synodality' imply in Lucy Esipila’s view regarding the church?

options.A: A rigid hierarchical structure that dictates church policies from the center.
options.B: An approach of collective engagement and listening to marginalized voices.
options.C: A focus on solving the debt burdens of African nations through financial aid.
options.D: The historical expansion of Catholic doctrine across the African continent.

questions.explanation: 文章第八段提到“synodality”被定义为“walking together”（共同行走），即作为全球教会倾听来自边缘地区的声音，强调协同与包容。

questions.stem: Which of the following best describes the core objective of the Pope’s trip as described by religious figures mentioned in the text?

options.A: To promote political change in Muslim-majority countries.
options.B: To increase the number of dioceses across the globe.
options.C: To foster harmony, peace, and fraternal living between different faiths.
options.D: To replace traditional theological practices with modern social thought.

questions.explanation: 文中多次提到教皇此行的目的，包括促进宗教间的和解、和平、包容以及人类友爱（fraternal living），特别是强调基督徒与穆斯林之间的和谐。
